export default "# User Guide - ResumeConverter\n\n## Table of Contents\n\n1. [Introduction](#introduction)\n2. [Quick Start](#quick-start)\n3. [User Profile](#user-profile)\n4. [Resume Management](#resume-management)\n5. [Missions](#missions)\n6. [Profile Matching](#profile-matching)\n7. [Selection Pipeline](#selection-pipeline)\n8. [Resume Adaptations](#resume-adaptations)\n9. [Clients & Prospects (CRM)](#clients--prospects)\n10. [Deals](#deals)\n11. [Email CV Sending](#email-cv-sending)\n12. [Market Radar](#market-radar)\n13. [AI Assistant](#ai-assistant)\n14. [Administration](#administration)\n15. [Database Backup](#database-backup)\n16. [GDPR Compliance](#gdpr-compliance)\n17. [Interface and Navigation](#interface-and-navigation)\n18. [Best Practices](#best-practices)\n19. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)\n20. [FAQ](#faq)\n21. [Roadmap](#roadmap)\n22. [Glossary](#glossary)\n23. [Support](#support)\n\n---\n\n## Introduction\n\n### What is ResumeConverter?\n\n**ResumeConverter** is an AI-powered resume management and optimization platform. Designed for staffing agencies and recruitment firms, it allows you to:\n\n- **Analyze** resumes with detailed AI evaluation\n- **Improve** resume quality and impact automatically\n- **Adapt** resumes for specific missions or positions\n- **Anonymize** resumes to protect candidate identity\n- **Track** performance with detailed scores\n- **Export** resumes to PDF format\n\n### Why Use ResumeConverter?\n\n- **Time savings**: Automated analysis and improvement\n- **ATS optimization**: Maximum compatibility with recruitment systems\n- **Personalization**: Intelligent adaptation for each opportunity\n- **Confidentiality**: Anonymous mode with trigram to protect identity\n- **Centralized tracking**: Manage all your resumes in one place\n\n---\n\n## Quick Start\n\n### First Login\n\n1. Access the application via your browser\n2. Log in with credentials provided by your administrator\n3. Explore the dashboard displaying your main statistics\n\n### Typical Workflow\n\n```\n1. Import a resume → 2. Analyze → 3. Improve → 4. Create a mission → 5. Adapt the resume → 6. Export\n```\n\n---\n\n## User Profile\n\nEach user has a personal space to manage their information and security settings.\n\n### Accessing Your Profile\n\n1. Click on your **name** or **avatar** in the top right corner\n2. Select **\"My Profile\"** from the dropdown menu\n\n### Profile Tab\n\nThe **Profile** tab allows you to view and edit your personal information:\n\n| Field | Description |\n|-------|-------------|\n| **Name** | Your full name |\n| **Email** | Your email address (login identifier) |\n| **Job Title** | Your position (used in email signatures) |\n| **Phone** | Your professional phone number |\n| **Firm** | Your assigned firm |\n\n### Security Tab\n\nThe **Security** tab allows you to configure two-factor authentication (2FA).\n\n#### What is Two-Factor Authentication?\n\nTwo-factor authentication (2FA) adds an extra layer of security to your account. In addition to your password, you'll need to enter a temporary code generated by an authenticator app on your phone.\n\n> **Important note**: 2FA only applies to email/password login. If you sign in via Google (OAuth2), your Google account security applies.\n\n#### Compatible Apps\n\nYou can use any of the following apps:\n- **Google Authenticator** (Android / iOS)\n- **Microsoft Authenticator** (Android / iOS)\n- **Authy** (Android / iOS / Desktop)\n- Any TOTP-compatible app (RFC 6238)\n\n#### Enabling 2FA\n\n1. Go to **My Profile** → **Security** tab\n2. Click **\"Enable 2FA\"**\n3. A QR code will be displayed\n4. Open your authenticator app\n5. Scan the QR code with the app\n6. Enter the 6-digit code shown by the app\n7. Click **\"Verify\"**\n\nOnce enabled, you'll receive **8 backup codes**. **Store them safely** in a secure location (password manager, safe, etc.). These codes will allow you to log in if you lose access to your authenticator app.\n\n#### Logging In with 2FA\n\n1. Enter your email and password as usual\n2. A 2FA verification screen appears\n3. Open your authenticator app\n4. Enter the 6-digit code displayed\n5. Click **\"Verify\"**\n\n> **Tip**: Codes change every 30 seconds. If your code is rejected, wait for the next one.\n\n#### Using a Backup Code\n\nIf you no longer have access to your authenticator app:\n\n1. On the 2FA verification screen, enter one of your backup codes (8 characters)\n2. Click **\"Verify\"**\n\n> **Warning**: Each backup code can only be used once.\n\n#### Regenerating Backup Codes\n\nIf you've used several backup codes or believe they've been compromised:\n\n1. Go to **My Profile** → **Security** tab\n2. Click **\"Regenerate backup codes\"**\n3. Enter a valid 2FA code to confirm\n4. New codes are generated (old ones are invalidated)\n5. **Save the new codes immediately**\n\n#### Disabling 2FA\n\n1. Go to **My Profile** → **Security** tab\n2. Click **\"Disable 2FA\"**\n3. Enter a valid 2FA code to confirm\n4. 2FA is disabled\n\n> **Recommendation**: We recommend keeping 2FA enabled for optimal account security.\n\n---\n\n## Resume Management\n\n### Importing a Resume\n\n#### Supported Formats\n\n- **PDF** (.pdf) - Recommended for best accuracy\n- **Microsoft Word** (.docx, .doc)\n- **Maximum size**: 50 MB\n\n#### OCR for scanned / image PDFs\n\nThe application can process **scanned PDFs** or image-based PDFs:\n\n1. native PDF text extraction is attempted first\n2. if the PDF has no usable text layer, the server switches to OCR\n3. AI analysis then runs on the extracted OCR text\n\nImportant notes:\n- a PDF with **selectable text** remains the most reliable case\n- a blurred, noisy, heavily compressed, or very small image PDF may still be recognized imperfectly\n- in Docker production, the recommended OCR path is based on `pdftoppm` + `tesseract`\n- outside Docker, your administrator must install the required OCR system binaries to get the same reliability level\n\n#### Import Process\n\n1. Go to **\"Resume Library\"** in the side menu\n2. Click the **\"Upload a new resume\"** button\n3. Drag and drop your file or click to select\n4. **Select the profile type**:\n   - **Employee**: Resume of your company's employee (no consent required)\n   - **External**: Resume of an external candidate (GDPR consent required)\n5. For **external** profiles, fill in the GDPR form:\n   - Candidate name\n   - Candidate email (for sending the consent request)\n6. Analysis starts automatically\n7. For external profiles, a consent request email is sent to the candidate\n\n> **Note**: For more details on GDPR consent management, see the [GDPR Compliance](#gdpr-compliance) section.\n\n#### What Happens After Import?\n\nThe AI automatically performs:\n\n1. **Text extraction**: Reading document content\n2. **Structural analysis**: Identifying sections (experience, education, etc.)\n3. **Skills detection**: Extracting technical and soft skills\n4. **Multi-criteria evaluation**: Assigning scores across 6 dimensions\n5. **Suggestion generation**: Improvement recommendations\n\n**Analysis time**: 10-30 seconds depending on resume complexity\n\n### Understanding Scores\n\nEach resume receives a detailed evaluation across **6 categories**:\n\n#### 1. Executive Summary\n- **Evaluates**: Hook quality, value proposition clarity\n- **Criteria**: Impact, conciseness, relevance\n\n#### 2. Skills\n- **Evaluates**: Skills presentation and relevance\n- **Criteria**: Organization, technical/soft skills balance, keywords\n\n#### 3. Professional Experience\n- **Evaluates**: Experience clarity and impact\n- **Criteria**: Quantified results, action verbs, progression\n\n#### 4. Education\n- **Evaluates**: Academic background presentation\n- **Criteria**: Clarity, relevance, certifications\n\n#### 5. ATS Compatibility\n- **Evaluates**: Ability to pass automated recruiter filters\n- **Criteria**: Keywords, formatting, structure\n\n#### 6. Hobbies & Languages\n- **Evaluates**: Additional information\n- **Criteria**: Relevance, language proficiency\n\n#### Global Score\n\nThe **global score** is a weighted average of the 6 categories. Weights can be configured by the administrator.\n\n**Interpretation**:\n- **80-100%**: Excellent - Optimized resume\n- **60-79%**: Good - Some improvements possible\n- **40-59%**: Average - Improvements recommended\n- **0-39%**: Low - Revision needed\n\n### Viewing a Resume\n\nClick on a resume in the **\"Resume Library\"** list to access its detailed view with multiple tabs:\n\n#### Overview Tab\n- Visual scores with radar charts\n- Main information (name, title, client)\n- Detected tags and skills\n- Quick actions (improve, export, delete)\n\n#### Skills & Tags Tab\n- Complete list of detected skills\n- Categorization: technical skills, soft skills, tools, industries\n\n#### Original Tab\n- View of extracted text from original resume\n- File metadata\n\n#### Improved Tab\n- AI-generated optimized version\n- **Rich editor** with integrated AI suggestions\n- **Suggestions panel**: Improvement recommendations by section\n- Option to re-improve the resume with AI\n- Export to PDF with template selection\n\n### Improving a Resume\n\n1. Open an analyzed resume\n2. Click the **\"Improve Resume\"** button\n3. The AI generates an optimized version with:\n   - Reformulation for greater impact\n   - ATS keyword optimization\n   - Achievement highlighting\n   - Professional structure\n\n**Note**: In anonymous mode, the improved resume will not contain personally identifiable information.\n\n### Editing and Refining an Improved Resume\n\nOnce the improved resume is generated, you can further customize it:\n\n#### Rich Editor\n\nThe Improved tab offers a complete **WYSIWYG editor**:\n- Text formatting (bold, italic, lists)\n- Direct content modification\n- Automatic saving of changes\n\n#### AI Suggestions Integrated in Text\n\nImprovement suggestions are displayed **directly inline** in the editor:\n\n- **Visual indicators**: Colored markers highlight passages to improve\n- **Contextual suggestions**: Hover or click on a marker to see the suggestion\n- **Easy application**: Click to apply the suggestion or dismiss it\n\nThis integration allows you to immediately see where and how to improve the resume without leaving the text context.\n\n#### Suggestions Panel by Section\n\nAdditionally, a side panel groups **suggestions by section**:\n\n| Section | Suggestion Types |\n|---------|------------------|\n| **Summary** | Reformulations for greater impact |\n| **Skills** | Missing keywords, organization |\n| **Experience** | Result quantification, action verbs |\n| **Education** | Certification highlighting |\n| **ATS** | Optimizations for automated filters |\n\n**Using suggestions**:\n1. Review suggestions in the side panel or directly in the text\n2. Click on a suggestion to apply it or use it as inspiration\n3. Manually edit the text in the editor if needed\n\n#### Re-improve with AI\n\nIf you want a new improved version:\n1. Click **\"Improve with AI\"** in the Improved tab\n2. The AI generates a new version based on the current content\n3. Scores are automatically recalculated\n\n### Exporting a Resume\n\n1. Open a resume (Improved tab)\n2. Click **\"Export\"**\n3. Choose the formatting template\n4. The PDF file downloads automatically\n\n### Batch Import (Administrators)\n\nBatch import allows **administrators** to upload multiple resumes simultaneously for automated processing.\n\n#### Accessing Batch Import\n\n1. Go to **\"Resume Library\"** in the sidebar\n2. Click the **\"Batch Import\"** button (visible only to administrators)\n\n#### Import Methods\n\n| Method | Description |\n|--------|-------------|\n| **Drag and drop** | Drag files or an entire folder into the drop zone |\n| **Select folder** | Click the \"Select folder\" button to choose a folder with subfolders |\n| **Click to select** | Click in the drop zone to select individual files |\n\n#### Folder Structure Preservation\n\nBatch import **automatically preserves the folder structure** during import and export:\n\n- **Display**: The relative path of each file is shown in gray before the file name\n- **Export**: The generated ZIP file reproduces the original folder structure\n\n**Example:**\n```\nImported folder:                     Exported ZIP:\nMyFolder/                            PDF/\n├── Consultants/                     ├── MyFolder/Consultants/\n│   ├── Senior/                      │   ├── Senior/\n│   │   └── CV_John.pdf              │   │   └── JDO_Template.pdf\n│   └── Junior/                      │   └── Junior/\n│       └── CV_Mary.pdf              │       └── MMA_Template.pdf\n```\n\n> **Note**: The exported file name follows the format `{trigram}_{template_name}.{extension}` where the trigram is extracted from the analyzed resume.\n\n#### Processing Options\n\n| Option | Description |\n|--------|-------------|\n| **Automatically improve** | AI improves each resume after analysis (adds ~45s per resume) |\n| **Export after processing** | Generates a ZIP file containing all exported resumes |\n| **Delete after processing** | Removes resumes from database after export (useful for one-time processing) |\n\n#### Export Settings\n\nIf the export option is enabled:\n- **Export template**: Select the formatting template\n- **Format**: PDF, DOCX, or both\n\n#### Import Process\n\n1. **Drag and drop** your files or folders, or use the **\"Select folder\"** button\n2. **Limit**: Maximum 100 files per batch\n3. **Max size**: 50 MB per file\n4. **Verify** the relative paths displayed for each file\n5. Configure **processing options**\n6. Click **\"Process X file(s)\"**\n7. Monitor progress in real-time\n\n#### Dedicated Jobs Screen\n\nThe **Jobs** screen allows you to track and manage all batch processing jobs:\n\n**Access:**\n- From the **Batch Upload** screen, after starting a processing job, you are automatically redirected to **Jobs**\n- From the lower sidebar menu: **Settings** → **Jobs** → **Backup**\n\n| Information | Description |\n|-------------|-------------|\n| **Status** | Pending, Processing, Completed, Failed, Cancelled |\n| **Progress** | Number of files processed / total |\n| **Success/Errors** | Counters for successful and failed files |\n| **Details** | List of files with their relative path and individual status |\n| **Export** | Download button for the ZIP file (if export enabled) |\n\n**Available actions:**\n- **Download**: Download the export ZIP file (completed jobs with export)\n- **Cancel**: Cancel a job in progress\n- **Delete**: Remove a job from history\n\n#### Progress Tracking\n\nFor each file, you can see:\n- **Relative path**: Original folder structure (in gray)\n- **File name**: Resume name\n- **Status**: Pending, Uploading, Extracting, Analyzing, Improving, Exporting, Done, Error\n- **Progress bar**: Processing advancement\n\n#### Error Handling\n\n- Failed files are flagged with a detailed error message\n- Processing continues for other files even if errors occur\n- Successful files are not affected by other files' errors\n\n#### GDPR\n\n> **Important**: Batch imported resumes are automatically treated as **internal (employees)**. No consent request is sent and no candidate name is recorded.\n\n### Resume Comments\n\nEach resume has a **Comments** section allowing users to collaborate and share information about a candidate.\n\n#### Adding a Comment\n\n1. Open a resume (analysis or improvement page)\n2. Scroll down to the **\"Comments\"** section at the bottom of the page\n3. Enter your comment in the text field\n4. Click **\"Add\"** to publish\n\n#### Comment Types\n\n| Type | Description | Visibility |\n|------|-------------|------------|\n| **Public** | Visible to all users | Everyone |\n| **Private** | Visible only to you | You only |\n\nTo create a private comment, check the **\"Private comment\"** option before publishing.\n\n#### Managing Your Comments\n\n- **Edit**: Click the edit icon to modify your comment\n- **Delete**: Click the delete icon to remove your comment\n\n> **Note**: You can only edit or delete your own comments.\n\n#### Use Cases\n\n- **Follow-up notes**: Document exchanges with the candidate\n- **Interview feedback**: Share impressions after an interview\n- **Additional information**: Add details not present in the resume\n- **Alerts**: Flag points of attention to colleagues\n\n### Sharing a Resume via QR Code\n\nThe **QR code sharing** feature allows you to easily share a resume with third parties (clients, managers, candidates) without them needing to log into the application.\n\n#### Sharing from the Analysis Page (Original Resume)\n\n1. Open an analyzed resume\n2. Click the **\"Share\"** button (share icon)\n3. A modal displays with:\n   - A scannable **QR code**\n   - A copyable **share URL**\n   - A button to **download the QR code** as an image\n\n> ⚠️ **Warning**: From the analysis page, you are sharing the **original version** of the resume, not the improved version. A warning message reminds you of this.\n\n#### Sharing from the Improvement Page (Improved Resume)\n\n1. Open an improved resume\n2. Click the **\"Share\"** button\n3. The application automatically generates a **PDF of the improved resume** with the selected template\n4. The QR code and share link are displayed\n\n#### Using the Share Link\n\nThe recipient can:\n- **Scan the QR code** with their smartphone\n- **Click the link** copied and shared via email or messaging\n\nThe resume displays directly in the browser:\n- **On desktop**: The PDF displays in the browser\n- **On mobile**: \"Open\" and \"Download\" buttons are provided\n\n#### Sharing Security\n\n- Share links use a **unique 64-character token**\n- Links are **persistent** and remain valid\n- No authentication is required to access the shared resume\n- Links cannot be guessed (cryptographically secure token)\n\n#### Use Cases\n\n| Situation | Recommended Action |\n|-----------|-------------------|\n| Send a resume to a client | Share from the improvement page (optimized resume) |\n| Allow candidate to verify their resume | Share from the analysis page (original resume) |\n| Presentation in a meeting | Display the QR code on screen for quick access |\n| Integration in an email | Copy the link and insert it in the message body |\n\n---\n\n## Missions\n\n### What is a Mission?\n\nA **mission** represents a professional opportunity (position, project, contract) for which you want to adapt resumes.\n\nA mission can be **associated with a deal** to organize commercial tracking. This association is optional: a mission can exist independently.\n\n### Creating a Mission\n\n1. Go to **\"Missions\"** in the menu\n2. Click **\"New Mission\"**\n3. Fill in the form:\n\n| Field | Description | Required |\n|-------|-------------|----------|\n| **Title** | Position title | Yes |\n| **Deal** | Associated deal (optional) | No |\n| **Client** | Client company | No |\n| **Contact** | Contact person at the client | No |\n| **Status** | Active, Draft or Closed | No |\n| **Description** | Detailed position description | Yes |\n\n#### Tips for an Effective Description\n\n**Include**:\n- Complete job description\n- Main responsibilities\n- Required technical skills\n- Desired soft skills\n- Company context\n\n**Avoid**:\n- Descriptions that are too short (< 100 words)\n- Vague or generic information\n\n### Managing Missions\n\nThe Missions page displays all your opportunities with:\n- Title and status\n- **Associated deal** (if linked to a deal)\n- Client and contact\n- Firm\n- Creation date\n- Actions (view, edit, delete)\n\n### Mission Detail Page\n\nBy clicking on a mission, you access its detail page which includes:\n\n#### Mission Information\n- **Title and client**: Header with main information\n- **Deal**: Link to the associated deal (if applicable)\n- **Description**: Detailed offer content\n- **Extracted keywords**: Technical skills, tools, industries, soft skills\n\n### Missions and Deals\n\nThe link between missions and deals allows you to:\n- **Group** multiple missions under the same deal\n- **View missions** directly in the CRM deals view (mission count displayed)\n- **View missions** in the \"By deal\" view of the CV library with adaptation counts\n- **Filter** missions by deal via the `?dealId=` query parameter\n\nA deal can contain **0 to N missions**, and a mission can be associated with **no deal**.\n\n#### Integrated Kanban Pipeline\n\nBelow the description, a **Kanban board** displays all candidates in the selection process for this mission:\n\n| Feature | Description |\n|---------|-------------|\n| **Columns** | One column per pipeline stage (New, Pre-selection, Submitted, Interview, etc.) |\n| **Candidate cards** | Name, score, tags, notes, date, interview count |\n| **Drag & Drop** | Drag a card to another column to change the stage |\n| **Counter** | Number of candidates per stage |\n\n#### Kanban Actions\n\n- **Add a candidate**: Button at the top to search and add a resume to the pipeline\n- **Manage interviews**: Camera icon to schedule/view interviews\n- **Edit notes**: Bubble icon to add comments\n- **View resume**: Eye icon to access the full profile\n- **Remove**: Trash icon to delete from pipeline\n\n> **Tip**: The Kanban allows you to quickly visualize the progress of all candidates for a mission and manage the selection process without leaving the page.\n\n---\n\n## Profile Matching\n\n### What is Profile Matching?\n\n**Profile Matching** is a powerful feature that automatically finds the best resumes in your database for a given mission. The AI analyzes the skills required by the mission and compares them to available profiles.\n\n### Accessing Profile Matching\n\n1. Go to **\"Profile Matching\"** in the sidebar menu\n2. Select a mission from the dropdown\n3. Click **\"Search\"**\n\n### How Does Matching Work?\n\n#### Step 1: Keyword Extraction\n\nDuring the first search for a mission, the AI automatically extracts keywords from the description:\n- **Technical skills**: Languages, frameworks, methodologies\n- **Tools**: Software, platforms, technologies\n- **Industries**: Sectors, areas of expertise\n- **Soft skills**: Personal qualities, behavioral competencies\n\nThese keywords are cached to speed up subsequent searches.\n\n#### Step 2: Profile Scoring\n\nEach resume is evaluated across 4 categories with configurable weights:\n\n| Category | Default Weight | Description |\n|----------|----------------|-------------|\n| **Skills** | 40% | Technical skills |\n| **Tools** | 25% | Software and technologies |\n| **Industries** | 20% | Sector experience |\n| **Soft Skills** | 15% | Personal qualities |\n\nThe initial score is a weighted average of these 4 categories.\n\n#### Step 3: Title-Based Refinement\n\nThe AI then analyzes each candidate's **job title** against the mission requirements:\n\n- A highly relevant title (e.g., \"Lead React Developer\" for a senior React mission) can add up to **+15 points**\n- A less relevant title (e.g., \"Project Manager\" for a pure technical mission) can subtract up to **-15 points**\n- A generic title (\"Consultant\", \"Engineer\") has a neutral impact\n\nThis adjustment helps better rank candidates whose profile matches the target position. The adjustment is visible on each profile card with a colored badge (green = bonus, red = penalty) and an explanation.\n\n### Customizing Weights\n\n1. Click **\"Advanced Options\"** below the mission selector\n2. Adjust the sliders for each category\n3. Weights automatically rebalance to total 100%\n4. Re-run the search to apply new weights\n\n### Understanding Results\n\n#### Profile Card\n\nEach found profile displays:\n- **Name and title** of the candidate\n- **Overall score** (match percentage)\n- **Category scores**: Skills, Tools, Industries, Soft Skills\n\n#### Profile Details\n\nClick on a card to see details:\n\n**Matched skills** (in green):\n- Resume tags that match mission requirements\n- Organized by category (Skills, Tools, Industries, Soft Skills)\n\n**Missing skills** (in red):\n- Tags required by the mission but absent from the resume\n- Helps identify candidate gaps\n\n### AI Detailed Analysis\n\nFor deeper insights, you can request an **in-depth AI analysis**:\n\n1. Click **\"Analyze profile\"** on a profile card\n2. The AI generates a comprehensive report including:\n\n| Section | Description |\n|---------|-------------|\n| **Verdict** | Overall assessment (Excellent match, Good match, etc.) |\n| **Summary** | 2-3 sentence synthesis of the fit |\n| **Strengths** | Candidate's assets for this mission |\n| **Gaps** | Missing skills with criticality level |\n| **Recommendations** | Advice for the candidate or recruiter |\n| **Interview Questions** | Suggested questions to validate uncertain points |\n| **Risk Level** | Recruitment risk assessment |\n\n#### Gap Criticality Levels\n\n- **Critical** (red): Essential skill missing\n- **Important** (orange): Important but not blocking skill\n- **Minor** (yellow): Secondary skill, easy to acquire\n\n#### Recommendation Types\n\n- **Highlight** (green): Points to emphasize during interview\n- **Develop** (blue): Skills to develop quickly\n- **Acquire** (purple): Recommended training or certifications\n\n### Available Actions\n\nFrom a profile, you can:\n- **Analyze profile**: Launch detailed AI analysis\n- **View Resume**: Access the full resume details\n\n### Refresh Keywords\n\nIf you modify a mission description, click the **\"Refresh\"** button (refresh icon) to force the AI to re-extract keywords.\n\n### Best Practices\n\n1. **Detailed descriptions**: The more detailed the mission, the more accurate the matching\n2. **Adjust weights**: Adapt weights according to the relative importance of criteria\n3. **Analyze top profiles**: Use detailed analysis for the top 3-5 candidates\n4. **Check gaps**: Missing skills can be discussion points during interviews\n\n---\n\n## Selection Pipeline\n\n### What is the Selection Pipeline?\n\nThe **Selection Pipeline** is a feature for managing the candidate selection process for your missions. It's a tracking tool that allows you to:\n\n- **Track progress** of each candidate in the selection process\n- **Schedule multiple interviews** successively with clients or partners\n- **Record history** of stages completed by each candidate\n- **Centralize notes** and comments on each application\n- **Visualize the pipeline** as a Kanban board at the mission level\n\n> **Note**: This pipeline is designed for selecting profiles (freelancers, partner employees) for client missions, not for internal recruitment.\n\n### Accessing the Pipeline\n\nThe pipeline is accessible in two ways:\n\n1. **From a Resume**: **\"Selection\"** tab on the analysis or improvement page\n2. **From a Mission**: Integrated Kanban view on each mission's detail page\n\n### Pipeline Stages\n\nThe pipeline includes 8 configured stages:\n\n| Stage | Description | Color |\n|-------|-------------|-------|\n| **New** | Candidate added to the process | Gray |\n| **Pre-selection** | Under internal evaluation | Blue |\n| **Submitted to client** | Resume sent to client | Purple |\n| **Interview scheduled** | Interview planned | Orange |\n| **Interview completed** | Interview finished | Light green |\n| **Selected** | Candidate retained | Green |\n| **Not retained** | Candidate not selected | Red |\n| **On hold** | Process paused | Orange |\n\n### Adding a Candidate to the Pipeline\n\n1. Open a resume and go to the **\"Selection\"** tab\n2. Click **\"Add to process\"**\n3. Optionally select:\n   - The associated **mission**\n   - The concerned **client**\n4. Add **notes** if needed\n5. Click **\"Add\"**\n\n### Managing Stages\n\n#### Changing a Candidate's Stage\n\n1. Click on the candidate card to expand it\n2. In the **\"Change stage\"** section, click on the desired stage\n3. History is automatically updated\n\n#### Adding Notes\n\nNotes allow you to document the follow-up:\n- Comments on the profile\n- Client feedback\n- Points of attention\n\n### Interview Scheduling\n\nThe pipeline allows scheduling **multiple successive interviews** for the same candidate on a mission. This reflects the real selection process which may include technical, HR, then client interviews.\n\n#### Scheduling an Interview\n\n1. Click the **camera icon** on the candidate card, or **\"Schedule an interview\"**\n2. Fill in the form:\n\n| Field | Description | Required |\n|-------|-------------|----------|\n| **Title** | Interview title | Yes |\n| **Type** | Client, Partner, Technical, HR | Yes |\n| **Date and time** | Interview moment | Yes |\n| **Duration** | 30 min to 2h | No |\n| **Location** | Address or \"Video call\" | No |\n| **Meeting link** | Google Meet, Teams URL, etc. | No |\n| **Description** | Additional details | No |\n\n3. Click **\"Schedule\"**\n\n#### Interview Types and Pipeline Impact\n\n| Type | Color | Changes stage? |\n|------|-------|----------------|\n| **Client interview** | Blue | ✅ Yes → \"Interview scheduled\" |\n| **Partner interview** | Purple | ❌ No |\n| **Technical interview** | Green | ❌ No |\n| **HR interview** | Orange | ❌ No |\n\n> **Important**: Only **\"Client\"** type interviews automatically move the candidate to the \"Interview scheduled\" stage. Other interview types (technical, HR, partner) are preparatory steps that don't modify the pipeline stage.\n\n#### Managing Multiple Interviews\n\nFrom a candidate's interview list, you can:\n- **View all interviews**: Past, scheduled, cancelled\n- **Add a new interview**: \"Schedule an interview\" button\n- **Mark as completed**: ✓ icon with outcome selection\n- **Cancel**: ✗ icon to cancel a scheduled interview\n\n#### Interview Types\n\n- **Client interview**: With the end client\n- **Partner interview**: With a partner company\n- **Technical interview**: Technical skills assessment\n- **HR interview**: HR validation interview\n\n#### Completing an Interview\n\n1. Click the ✓ (check) icon next to the interview\n2. Select the **outcome**:\n   - **Positive**: Successful interview\n   - **Neutral**: To be evaluated\n   - **Negative**: Unsuccessful\n   - **Follow-up needed**: Requires follow-up\n3. Add **notes** about the outcome\n4. Click **\"Complete\"**\n\n#### Canceling an Interview\n\nClick the ✗ (cross) icon to cancel a scheduled interview.\n\n### Google Calendar Integration\n\n#### Connecting Your Calendar\n\n1. Go to **Settings** > **Calendar**\n2. Click **\"Connect Google Calendar\"**\n3. Authorize access to your Google calendar\n4. Once connected, interviews will automatically be added to your calendar\n\n#### Calendar Features\n\n- **Automatic creation**: Scheduled interviews create an event in your calendar\n- **Invitations**: Participants receive an email invitation\n- **Reminders**: Notifications 24h and 30 min before the interview\n- **Synchronization**: Changes are reflected in the calendar\n\n### Pipeline History\n\nClick **\"View history\"** to see all stages completed by a candidate:\n- Date and time of each change\n- Previous and new stage\n- User who made the change\n- Associated notes\n\n### Removing a Candidate\n\nTo remove a candidate from the pipeline:\n1. Expand the candidate card\n2. Click **\"Remove from pipeline\"**\n3. Confirm the deletion\n\n> **Warning**: This action also deletes the history and associated interviews.\n\n### Kanban View at Mission Level\n\nThe mission detail page displays a **Kanban board** allowing you to visualize all pipeline candidates for that mission.\n\n#### Kanban Features\n\n- **Columns by stage**: Each column represents a pipeline stage\n- **Candidate cards**: Display name, score, tags, notes, date, interview count\n- **Drag & Drop**: Drag and drop cards between columns to change stage\n- **Counter**: Number of candidates per stage shown in the header\n\n#### Card Actions\n\n| Icon | Action |\n|------|--------|\n| 📹 Camera | Manage interviews |\n| 💬 Bubble | Edit notes |\n| 👁 Eye | View full resume |\n| 🗑 Trash | Remove from pipeline |\n\n#### Adding a Candidate from the Mission\n\n1. Click **\"Add candidate\"** at the top of the Kanban\n2. Search for a resume in the list\n3. Select the desired resume\n4. Add optional notes\n5. Click **\"Add\"**\n\nThe candidate appears in the \"New\" column.\n\n### Best Practices\n\n1. **Document each stage**: Add notes with each stage change\n2. **Schedule quickly**: Create interviews as soon as they're confirmed\n3. **Complete interviews**: Mark interviews as completed with an outcome\n4. **Use types**: Categorize your interviews for better tracking\n5. **Connect your calendar**: Avoid double entries and forgotten appointments\n6. **Use the Kanban**: Visualize overall progress on the mission page\n\n---\n\n## Resume Adaptations\n\n### What is an Adaptation?\n\nAn **adaptation** is a customized version of a resume, optimized for a specific mission. The AI analyzes the match between the profile and the offer, then generates a tailored resume.\n\n### Creating an Adaptation\n\n#### From a Resume\n\n1. Open a resume in the Resume Library\n2. Click **\"Adapt to a mission\"**\n3. Select the target mission\n4. Click **\"Generate adaptation\"**\n\n#### From a Mission\n\n1. Open a mission\n2. Click **\"Adapt a resume\"**\n3. Select the resume to adapt\n4. Click **\"Generate adaptation\"**\n\n### Match Analysis\n\nThe AI performs a multi-step analysis:\n\n1. **Skills Matching**: Comparison with required skills\n2. **Contextual Analysis**: Evaluation of relevant experience\n3. **Match Score**: Score from 0 to 100\n\n**Score interpretation**:\n- **80-100**: Excellent match\n- **60-79**: Good match\n- **40-59**: Average match\n- **0-39**: Low match\n\n### Adaptation Content\n\n- **Adapted Resume**: Customized version with reformulated summary, reorganized experiences, aligned skills\n- **Analysis Report**: Detailed score, strengths, areas for improvement\n\n---\n\n## Clients & Prospects\n\n### What is Clients & Prospects Management?\n\nThe **Clients & Prospects** feature (accessible via the **CRM** tab in the sidebar) allows you to manage your commercial portfolio: client companies, prospects, their contacts, and **deals** (business opportunities). This information is used for tracking resume submissions, personalizing emails, and organizing your resumes by opportunity.\n\n### Accessing the CRM\n\n1. Go to **\"CRM\"** in the sidebar menu\n2. The CRM area offers three tabs:\n   - **Clients & Prospects**: Manage your companies and contacts\n   - **Deals**: Manage your business opportunities (see dedicated section below)\n   - **Dashboard**: Overview of your commercial activity\n\n### Company Types\n\n| Type | Description | Icon |\n|------|-------------|------|\n| **Client** | Company with an established business relationship | Blue |\n| **Prospect** | Potential company, in prospecting phase | Orange |\n\n### Creating a Client or Prospect\n\n1. Click **\"Add a client/prospect\"**\n2. Fill in the form:\n\n| Field | Description | Required |\n|-------|-------------|----------|\n| **Name** | Company name | Yes |\n| **Type** | Client or Prospect | Yes |\n| **Sector** | Industry sector (Banking, Healthcare, etc.) | No |\n\n3. Click **\"Save\"**\n\n### Managing Contacts\n\nEach client/prospect can have multiple contacts (interlocutors).\n\n#### Adding a Contact\n\n1. Open a client/prospect profile\n2. In the **\"Contacts\"** section, click **\"Add a contact\"**\n3. Fill in the information:\n\n| Field | Description | Required |\n|-------|-------------|----------|\n| **Name** | Contact's full name | Yes |\n| **Email** | Professional email address | Yes |\n| **Role** | Position held (HR Director, Manager, etc.) | No |\n| **Phone** | Phone number | No |\n\n#### Editing or Deleting a Contact\n\n- Click the **pencil** icon to edit\n- Click the **trash** icon to delete\n\n### Submission History\n\nFor each client/prospect, you can view the history of submitted resumes:\n\n1. Open the client/prospect profile\n2. Check the **\"Submissions\"** section\n3. View submitted resumes with date, recipient contact, and status\n\n### Best Practices\n\n- **Qualify your contacts**: Fill in the role to personalize your emails\n- **Update regularly**: Remove obsolete contacts\n- **Convert your prospects**: Change the type to \"Client\" after signing\n- **Create deals**: Associate your resumes with deals for structured tracking\n\n---\n\n## Deals\n\n### What is a Deal?\n\nA **deal** represents a business opportunity or client need to which you can associate resumes. Deals allow you to organize your candidates by business context and track the progress of each opportunity.\n\n### Accessing Deals\n\n1. Go to **\"CRM\"** in the sidebar menu\n2. Click the **\"Deals\"** tab\n\n### Creating a Deal\n\n1. Click **\"New Deal\"**\n2. Fill in the form:\n\n| Field | Description | Required |\n|-------|-------------|----------|\n| **Title** | Deal title (e.g., \"IT Migration - HSBC\") | Yes |\n| **Client** | Associated client or prospect | No |\n| **Contact** | Contact person at the client | No |\n| **Status** | Open, Won, Lost, On Hold | Yes |\n| **Priority** | Low, Medium, High, Urgent | Yes |\n| **Description** | Opportunity details | No |\n\n3. Click **\"Save\"**\n\n### Deal Statuses\n\n| Status | Description | Color |\n|--------|-------------|-------|\n| **Open** | Active deal, in negotiation | Blue |\n| **Won** | Successfully closed deal | Green |\n| **Lost** | Deal not retained | Red |\n| **On Hold** | Temporarily suspended deal | Yellow |\n\n### Priorities\n\n| Priority | Indicator |\n|----------|-----------|\n| **Low** | ○ Gray |\n| **Medium** | ● Blue |\n| **High** | ●● Orange |\n| **Urgent** | ●●● Red |\n\n### Associating Resumes with a Deal\n\nThere are several ways to associate a resume with a deal:\n\n#### From the Resume Library (List View)\n\n1. On a resume card, click the **briefcase** icon (manage deals)\n2. Select one or more deals from the list\n3. The resume is immediately associated\n\n#### From the Resume Library (By Deal View)\n\n1. Switch to **\"By deal\"** view using the toggle at the top of the resume library\n2. **Drag and drop** a resume from any section onto a deal\n3. The resume is automatically moved/added to the target deal\n\n#### From a Deal Profile (CRM)\n\n1. Open a deal in the **\"Deals\"** tab of the CRM\n2. Use the **\"Add a resume\"** button to search and associate resumes\n\n### By Deal View in the Resume Library\n\nThe resume library offers two display modes, accessible via a toggle at the top of the page:\n\n#### List Mode (Default)\n\nThe classic mode: all resumes displayed in a paginated list with filters and search.\n\n#### By Deal Mode\n\nThis mode organizes resumes by deal, with:\n\n- **Summary bar**: Total number of deals, assigned resumes, and unassigned resumes\n- **Collapsible sections**: Each deal appears as an accordion showing title, status, priority, client, and contact\n- **Counter badge**: Number of resumes associated with each deal\n- **\"Unassigned\" section**: Resumes not associated with any deal appear in a dedicated section at the bottom\n\n#### Drag & Drop\n\nThe **By deal** mode supports **drag and drop** of resumes between sections:\n\n1. **Click and hold** on a resume card\n2. **Drag** the resume onto the target deal section\n3. The drop zone highlights in purple with a \"Drop here\" indicator\n4. **Release** to confirm the move\n\n**Behaviors:**\n- **Unassigned resume → Deal**: The resume is added to the deal\n- **Deal A → Deal B**: The resume is removed from A and added to B\n- A confirmation toast appears after each operation\n- The expanded/collapsed state of sections is preserved after a move\n\n### Best Practices\n\n- **Name your deals clearly**: Use a consistent format (e.g., \"Project - Client\")\n- **Update statuses**: Mark a deal as \"Won\" or \"Lost\" for reliable tracking\n- **Use priorities**: Urgent deals appear first in the by-deal view\n- **Drag & drop to organize**: Use the by-deal view to quickly reassign your resumes\n\n---\n\n## Email CV Sending\n\n### Overview\n\nThe **email CV sending** feature allows you to create professional email drafts directly from the application, with the resume attached. Emails are personalized using templates and dynamic keywords.\n\n### Prerequisites\n\n1. **Gmail connection**: Connect your Gmail account to create drafts\n2. **Client/Prospect**: Create at least one client or prospect with a contact\n3. **Email template**: A default template is available, or create your own\n\n### Connecting Gmail\n\n1. Open a resume in the Resume Library\n2. Click **\"Send by email\"**\n3. Click **\"Connect Gmail\"**\n4. Authorize the application to access your Gmail account\n5. Once connected, your email address is displayed\n\n### Sending a Resume\n\n#### Step 1: Open the Send Modal\n\n1. Open a resume (Improved tab)\n2. Click the **\"Send by email\"** button (envelope icon)\n\n#### Step 2: Select the Recipient\n\n1. Choose a **Client or Prospect** from the dropdown\n2. Select a **Contact** from those associated with the client\n3. The contact's email address is displayed automatically\n\n#### Step 3: Choose the Template\n\n1. Select an **email template** from the list\n2. Click **\"Preview\"** to see the rendering with your data\n3. The subject and body are generated automatically\n\n#### Step 4: Create the Draft\n\n1. Verify the information\n2. Click **\"Create draft\"**\n3. The draft is created in your Gmail inbox\n4. Open Gmail to review and send the email\n\n### Email Templates\n\n#### What is a Template?\n\nAn **email template** is a pre-formatted message model with dynamic keywords that are automatically replaced with actual values when sending.\n\n#### Available Keywords\n\n| Category | Keyword | Description |\n|----------|---------|-------------|\n| **Client** | `{{client.name}}` | Client/prospect name |\n| **Client** | `{{client.type}}` | Type (Client or Prospect) |\n| **Contact** | `{{contact.name}}` | Contact's full name |\n| **Contact** | `{{contact.firstName}}` | Contact's first name |\n| **Contact** | `{{contact.role}}` | Contact's position |\n| **Resume** | `{{resume.name}}` | Candidate name |\n| **Resume** | `{{resume.title}}` | Candidate's job title |\n| **Resume** | `{{resume.version}}` | Resume version number |\n| **Firm** | `{{firm.name}}` | Your firm's name |\n| **Firm** | `{{firm.logo}}` | Your firm's logo |\n| **User** | `{{user.name}}` | Your name |\n| **User** | `{{user.email}}` | Your email |\n| **User** | `{{user.jobTitle}}` | Your job title |\n| **User** | `{{user.phone}}` | Your phone |\n| **Date** | `{{date.today}}` | Today's date (short format) |\n| **Date** | `{{date.todayLong}}` | Today's date (long format) |\n\n### Managing Templates (Admin)\n\nAdministrators can create and manage email templates.\n\n#### Accessing Template Management\n\n1. Go to **\"Email Templates\"** in the admin menu\n2. View the list of existing templates\n\n#### Creating a Template\n\n1. Click **\"New template\"**\n2. Fill in the form:\n\n| Field | Description |\n|-------|-------------|\n| **Name** | Template name (e.g., \"Standard application\") |\n| **Subject** | Email subject with keywords |\n| **Content** | Email body with visual blocks |\n\n#### Visual Template Editor\n\nThe editor offers **blocks** to structure your email:\n\n| Block | Description | Usage |\n|-------|-------------|-------|\n| **Logo** | Your firm's logo | Email header |\n| **Header** | Title or firm name | Identification |\n| **Paragraph** | Free text | Message body |\n| **Signature** | Signature block | Sender's contact info |\n| **Footer** | Additional information | Date, legal notices |\n\n---\n\n## Market Radar\n\n### What is Market Radar?\n\n**Market Radar** is a market intelligence and analysis tool for the IT job market in France. It allows you to visualize trends, salaries, job offers, and tensions by region and profession.\n\n### Accessing Market Radar\n\n1. Go to **\"Market Radar\"** in the sidebar menu\n2. Choose the desired tab: Map, Data, Trends, or Professions\n\n### Market Radar Tabs\n\n#### France Map Tab\n\nInteractive data visualization by region:\n\n- **Data type selection**: Tension, Salary, Offers, Employment dynamics, Job seekers\n- **Interactive map**: Click on a region to see details\n- **Breakdown by profession**: List of professions with their indicators for the selected region\n- **On-demand details**: Click on a profession to see detailed metadata\n\n**Available data types**:\n\n| Type | Description |\n|------|-------------|\n| **Tension** | Ratio of offers to job seekers (higher = more in-demand profession) |\n| **Salary** | Median or average salary by profession and region |\n| **Offers** | Number of available job offers |\n| **Employment dynamics** | Evolution of employment numbers |\n| **Job seekers** | Number of job seekers |\n\n#### Data Tab\n\nDetailed table of collected market data:\n\n- **Filters**: Source, Region, Keyword/Profession\n- **Pagination**: Navigate through results\n- **Export**: View raw data\n\n#### Trends Tab\n\nMarket trends analysis:\n\n- **Time evolution**: Trend charts\n- **Regional comparison**: Differences between regions\n- **Key indicators**: Summary of important metrics\n\n#### Professions Tab\n\nList of referenced IT professions:\n\n- **ROME 4.0 Reference**: Official France Travail professions\n- **Associated skills**: Know-how and soft skills\n- **Challenges**: Profession challenges and opportunities\n\n### Data Collection (Admin)\n\nAdministrators can trigger data collection:\n\n1. Click the **\"Collect\"** button\n2. Select sources to query\n3. Wait for collection to complete\n4. New data appears in the tabs\n\n**Data sources**:\n- France Travail (offers, job seekers, tensions)\n- IT market studies\n- Salary data\n\n### Best Practices\n\n- **Check regularly**: Data is updated periodically\n- **Compare regions**: Identify geographical opportunities\n- **Analyze tensions**: In-demand professions offer more opportunities\n- **Cross-reference with your resumes**: Use these insights for profile matching\n\n---\n\n## AI Assistant\n\n### Accessing the Assistant\n\nThe AI assistant is available via the **chat button** (bubble icon) at the bottom right of the screen.\n\n**Note**: The assistant can be disabled by an administrator. If you don't see the button, contact your administrator.\n\n### Features\n\n#### Contextual Help\n- Explanations of application features\n- Step-by-step task guidance\n- Answers to frequently asked questions\n\n#### Personalized Advice\n- Recommendations for improving your resumes\n- Skills suggestions\n- Writing tips\n\n### Example Questions\n\n**About usage**:\n- \"How do I analyze a resume?\"\n- \"How do I create an adaptation?\"\n- \"How do I export to PDF?\"\n\n**About scores**:\n- \"Why is my ATS score low?\"\n- \"How can I improve the skills score?\"\n\n**Technical questions**:\n- \"What file formats are supported?\"\n- \"How long does analysis take?\"\n\n### Limitations\n\nThe assistant is designed to answer questions about **ResumeConverter** only. It cannot:\n- Answer off-topic questions\n- Give general career advice\n- Write complete resumes\n- Access your personal data\n\n---\n\n## Administration\n\n*These features are reserved for users with the \"Admin\" role.*\n\n### User Management\n\n#### Creating a User\n\n1. Go to **\"Users\"** in the admin menu\n2. Click **\"New user\"**\n3. Fill in: name, email, password, role, client\n\n#### Roles and Permissions\n\n| Role | Permissions |\n|------|-------------|\n| **User** | Manage own resumes, missions, adaptations |\n| **Admin** | All permissions + user management, templates, settings |\n\n### Resume Template Management\n\nTemplates define the structure and style of exported PDF resumes. Each template contains a header, body, and footer, along with a CSS stylesheet.\n\n#### Accessing Templates\n\n1. Go to **\"Resume Templates\"** in the main menu\n2. View the list of available templates with their preview\n3. Use the search to filter templates\n\n#### Creating a Template Manually\n\n1. Click **\"New Template\"**\n2. Fill in the form:\n\n| Field | Description |\n|-------|-------------|\n| **Name** | Template name (e.g., \"Corporate Blue Template\") |\n| **Description** | Description of the style and usage |\n| **Header** | HTML for the header (logo, company name) |\n| **Body** | HTML for the body with placeholders |\n| **Footer** | HTML for the footer (pagination, legal mentions) |\n| **Stylesheet** | CSS for visual styling |\n\n3. Use the TinyMCE visual editor for each section\n4. Click **\"Save\"**\n\n#### Required Placeholders\n\nThe template body must contain these 3 placeholders that will be automatically replaced:\n\n| Placeholder | Description |\n|-------------|-------------|\n| `-name-` | Candidate's full name |\n| `-title-` | Candidate's title or position |\n| `-content-` | Full CV content (experience, skills, etc.) |\n\n**Minimal body example**:\n```html\n<div class=\"cv-container\">\n  <h1 class=\"candidate-name\">-name-</h1>\n  <h2 class=\"candidate-title\">-title-</h2>\n  <div class=\"cv-content\">-content-</div>\n</div>\n```\n\n#### Extract a Template from an Existing CV (AI)\n\nThis feature allows you to automatically create a template from an existing CV by analyzing its visual structure with AI.\n\n**How it works:**\n\n1. On the **\"Resume Templates\"** page, click the purple **\"Extract\"** button (sparkles icon)\n2. A modal window opens\n3. **Drag and drop** a CV in PDF or DOCX format, or click to select a file\n4. Click **\"Extract Template\"**\n5. The AI analyzes the CV and extracts:\n   - Header structure (logo, company contact info)\n   - Visual style (colors, fonts, layout)\n   - Footer structure\n   - CSS stylesheet\n6. A **preview** of the extracted template is displayed\n7. Click **\"Create Template\"** to open the pre-filled editor\n8. Adjust the template if needed and save\n\n**Important notes:**\n- The AI extracts only the **visual structure**, not the CV content\n- Candidate's personal information is replaced with placeholders `-name-`, `-title-`, `-content-`\n- Images/logos are replaced with text placeholders `[COMPANY LOGO]`\n- The AI model used is the one configured in system settings\n- Always review and adjust the result before saving\n\n**Supported formats:** PDF, DOCX (max 10 MB)\n\n#### Editing a Template\n\n1. Click the **pencil** icon on the template card\n2. Edit the desired sections\n3. Click **\"Save\"**\n\n#### Deleting a Template\n\n1. Click the **trash** icon on the template card\n2. Confirm the deletion\n\n#### Previewing a Template\n\n1. Click the **eye** icon on the template card\n2. A preview of the template is displayed with visible placeholders\n\n### System Settings\n\n#### LLM Model Tab\n\n**AI Model Selection**:\n- GPT-4o (OpenAI) - Recommended\n- GPT-4o-mini (OpenAI) - Faster\n- Claude 3.5 Sonnet (Anthropic)\n- Other available models\n\n**CV Mode**:\n- **Nominative**: Resumes retain all candidate personal information\n- **Anonymous**: Personal information is replaced with a trigram (e.g., \"JDO\" for John Doe)\n\nAnonymous mode automatically removes:\n- First and last name (replaced by trigram)\n- Email addresses\n- Phone numbers\n- LinkedIn, GitHub, portfolio links\n- Postal address\n\n#### Weighting Tab\n\nAdjust the importance of each criterion in the global score:\n\n| Category | Default Weight |\n|----------|----------------|\n| Executive Summary | 20% |\n| Skills | 20% |\n| Experience | 20% |\n| Education | 15% |\n| ATS | 15% |\n| Hobbies & Languages | 10% |\n\n#### Chatbot Tab\n\nEnable or disable the AI assistant for all users.\n\n#### GDPR Tab\n\nConfigure GDPR consent email sending:\n\n1. **Connect Gmail**: Authorize the application to send emails via your Gmail account\n2. **Test sending**: Send a test email to verify the configuration\n3. **Connection status**: View the Gmail connection status\n\n> **Recommendation**: Use a dedicated Gmail account for GDPR emails (e.g., `gdpr@yourcompany.com`), different from your SSO login account.\n\nFor more details, see the [GDPR Compliance](#gdpr-compliance) section.\n\n### Security Logs\n\nView security events:\n- Successful and failed logins\n- Permission changes\n- Sensitive actions\n\n### Metrics\n\nView usage statistics:\n- Number of analyzed resumes\n- Adaptations created\n- LLM token usage\n\n---\n\n## Database Backup\n\nThe **Backup** page allows you to configure and manage automatic database backups to a remote FTP or SFTP server.\n\n### Accessing the Backup Page\n\n1. Go to **\"Backup\"** in the sidebar menu (Administration section)\n2. The page displays connection settings and backup history\n\n### Connection Configuration\n\nConfigure the backup server connection settings:\n\n| Parameter | Description |\n|-----------|-------------|\n| **Protocol** | FTP or SFTP |\n| **Host** | Server address (e.g., `ftp.example.com`) |\n| **Port** | Connection port (21 for FTP, 22 for SFTP) |\n| **Username** | FTP/SFTP username |\n| **Password** | Connection password |\n| **Remote Directory** | Destination folder on the server (e.g., `/backups`) |\n| **TLS Mode** | For FTP: Explicit (AUTH TLS), Implicit (port 990), or None |\n\n> **Security Recommendation**: Prefer SFTP or FTP with explicit TLS to encrypt transfers.\n\n### Testing the Connection\n\nBefore enabling scheduled backups:\n\n1. Fill in the connection settings\n2. Click **\"Test Connection\"**\n3. Verify that the test shows \"Connection successful\"\n\n### Scheduled Backups\n\nConfigure up to three types of automatic backups:\n\n| Type | Frequency | Configuration |\n|------|-----------|---------------|\n| **Daily** | Every day | Execution time (e.g., 02:00) |\n| **Weekly** | Once a week | Day and time (e.g., Sunday 03:00) |\n| **Monthly** | Once a month | Day of month and time (e.g., 1st at 04:00) |\n\nFor each type:\n1. Enable the backup with the toggle\n2. Configure the execution time\n3. Set the **retention** (number of backups to keep)\n\n> **Tip**: Schedule backups during off-peak hours (night) to minimize performance impact.\n\n### Manual Backup\n\nTo perform an immediate backup:\n\n1. Click **\"Backup Now\"**\n2. Select the backup type (daily, weekly, or monthly)\n3. The backup starts and its status appears in the history\n\n### Backup History\n\nThe history displays all completed backups:\n\n| Column | Description |\n|--------|-------------|\n| **Date** | Backup date and time |\n| **Type** | Daily, Weekly, or Monthly |\n| **File** | Backup filename |\n| **Size** | Compressed file size |\n| **Status** | Success, Running, or Failed |\n\n### Restoration\n\nTo restore a backup:\n\n1. Locate the backup in the history\n2. Click the **\"Restore\"** icon\n3. Confirm the restoration\n\n> **⚠️ Warning**: Restoration replaces all current data with the backup data. This action is irreversible.\n\n### Best Practices\n\n- **Test regularly** the FTP/SFTP connection\n- **Check the history** to ensure backups are running correctly\n- **Keep multiple versions** (retention of at least 7 days for daily backups)\n- **Test restoration** periodically on a test environment\n- **Monitor disk space** on the backup server\n\n---\n\n## GDPR Compliance\n\nResumeConverter includes a comprehensive GDPR consent management system for external candidate resumes, ensuring compliance with the General Data Protection Regulation.\n\n### Fundamental Principles\n\n#### Profile Types\n\nThe application distinguishes two profile types when importing a resume:\n\n| Type | Description | Consent Required |\n|------|-------------|------------------|\n| **Employee** | Your company's employee | Not required |\n| **External** | External candidate (freelance, consultant, etc.) | **Required** |\n\n#### Why Consent?\n\nGDPR requires obtaining explicit consent from individuals whose personal data you process. A resume contains many personal data points:\n- Name, contact information\n- Professional history\n- Education\n- Skills\n\n#### Data Processing Activities\n\nWhen a candidate gives consent, the following processing activities may be performed on their resume:\n\n| Processing | Description |\n|------------|-------------|\n| **Storage** | Resume is kept in the talent pool to contact the candidate |\n| **Analysis** | Automated extraction of skills, experience, and keywords |\n| **Improvement** | AI-powered reformulation and improvement of the resume |\n| **Adaptation** | Adapting the resume to a specific opportunity (mission) |\n| **Evaluation** | Calculating a match score between profile and opportunity |\n\n> **Important**: These automated processes help identify suitable opportunities for the candidate's profile. They do not, by themselves, produce an automated decision concerning the candidate. Human review is always involved.\n\n### Importing an External Resume\n\n#### Import Process\n\n1. **File selection**: Drag and drop or select the resume\n2. **GDPR form**: Fill in candidate information\n   - **Candidate name** (required)\n   - **Candidate email** (required for sending the request)\n3. **Automatic sending**: A consent request email is sent to the candidate\n\n#### Consent Email\n\nThe email sent to the candidate contains:\n- Your company's identity\n- **Why we store and process the resume** (detailed purposes)\n- **Data concerned** (resume, contact details, generated analyses)\n- **Data sharing** (no transmission to third parties without agreement)\n- **Retention period** (2 years maximum)\n- **Candidate rights** (access, rectification, deletion, etc.)\n- A link to **accept** consent\n- A link to **refuse** consent\n- **DPO email** to exercise rights\n\n**Response deadline**: The candidate has **14 days** to respond.\n\n### Consent Statuses\n\nThe GDPR badge displayed on each resume indicates its status:\n\n| Badge | Status | Description |\n|-------|--------|-------------|\n| 🟢 **Consented** | `active` | Candidate accepted data processing |\n| 🟡 **Pending** | `pending_consent` | Request sent, awaiting response |\n| 🔴 **Refused** | `refused` | Candidate refused - Resume will be deleted |\n| 🟠 **Expired** | `expired` | Response deadline passed - Resume will be deleted |\n| ⚪ **Not Required** | `not_required` | Internal employee resume |\n| 🔴 **Error** | `error` | Email sending failed |\n\n### GDPR Badge\n\n#### Display\n\nThe GDPR badge is visible:\n- In the **resume list** (CV Library)\n- On the resume **analysis** page\n- On the resume **improvement** page\n- On the resume **export** page\n\n#### Detailed Tooltip\n\nHovering over the badge displays detailed information:\n- **Candidate name**\n- **Candidate email**\n- **Response deadline** (for pending requests)\n- **Retention date** (for active consents)\n\n### Automatic Management\n\n#### Automatic Deletion\n\nThe application automatically manages resume lifecycle:\n\n1. **Consent refused**: Resume deleted within 24 hours\n2. **Deadline expired**: If candidate doesn't respond within 14 days, resume is deleted\n3. **Retention end**: At expiration of retention period (2 years by default)\n\n#### Automatic Reminders\n\nA reminder email is automatically sent if the candidate hasn't responded after 7 days.\n\n### DPO Configuration (Data Protection Officer)\n\nThe DPO is the contact point for candidates wishing to exercise their GDPR rights. Their contact details are included in consent emails.\n\n#### Configuration\n\n1. Go to **Settings** → **DPO**\n2. Fill in DPO information:\n   - **DPO Name**: Full name of the officer\n   - **DPO Email**: Contact email address (e.g., `dpo@yourcompany.com`)\n   - **DPO Phone**: Phone number (optional)\n3. Click **Save settings**\n\n> **Important**: The DPO email will be displayed in consent emails sent to candidates. Make sure it's a monitored email address capable of handling rights exercise requests.\n\n### Gmail GDPR Configuration\n\nTo send consent emails, you must connect a Gmail account.\n\n#### Connection\n\n1. Go to **Settings** → **GDPR**\n2. Click **Connect Gmail**\n3. Authorize the application to send emails on your behalf\n4. Test sending with the **Send test email** button\n\n#### Dedicated Account Recommended\n\n**Important**: We recommend using a dedicated Gmail account for GDPR emails (e.g., `gdpr@yourcompany.com`), different from your SSO login account, to avoid OAuth token conflicts.\n\n#### Troubleshooting\n\nIf email sending fails with \"Error\" status:\n1. Check Gmail connection in **Settings → GDPR**\n2. If status shows \"Reconnection required\", click **Reconnect Gmail**\n3. Retry sending by clicking **Resend request** in the GDPR badge\n\n### GDPR Best Practices\n\n#### Before Import\n\n- ✅ Verify you have the correct candidate email\n- ✅ Inform the candidate they will receive a consent request\n- ✅ Use a dedicated Gmail account for GDPR emails\n\n#### Consent Tracking\n\n- ✅ Regularly check resumes pending consent\n- ✅ Manually follow up if needed (\"Resend\" button)\n- ✅ Don't use a resume until consent is obtained\n\n#### Data Retention\n\n- ✅ Respect the defined retention period (2 years by default)\n- ✅ Resumes are automatically deleted at expiration\n- ✅ Candidates can request deletion at any time\n\n### Candidate Rights\n\nUnder GDPR, candidates have the following rights:\n\n| Right | Description | Implementation |\n|-------|-------------|----------------|\n| **Access** | View their data | Contact DPO |\n| **Rectification** | Correct their data | Contact DPO |\n| **Erasure** | Delete their data | Link in email or contact DPO |\n| **Objection** | Refuse processing | \"Refuse\" link in email |\n| **Portability** | Retrieve their data | Contact DPO |\n\n### GDPR Audit Log\n\nThe application maintains a comprehensive log of all GDPR actions, accessible to administrators via **Admin → GDPR Audit Log**.\n\n#### Logged Actions\n\nAll GDPR-related actions are automatically recorded:\n\n| Category | Actions |\n|----------|---------|\n| **Consent** | Request sent, reminder, acceptance, refusal, expiration |\n| **Data** | Export, deletion, anonymization |\n| **CV** | Upload, processing, purge, access |\n| **Automated** | Scheduled purges, automatic reminders |\n| **Admin** | Settings changes, DPO configuration |\n\n#### Recorded Information\n\nFor each action, the log records:\n- **Date and time** of the action\n- **Action type** (consent sent, purge, etc.)\n- **Firm** concerned (for multi-firm environments)\n- **Target**: candidate name and email\n- **Type**: manual or automated action\n- **Details**: additional information (retention period, reason, etc.)\n\n#### Viewing the Log\n\n1. Go to **Admin → GDPR Audit Log**\n2. Use **filters** to refine your search:\n   - By **firm**\n   - By **category** (consent, data, CV, etc.)\n   - By specific **action type**\n   - By **type** (automated or manual)\n   - By candidate **email**\n   - By **date range** (start and end dates)\n3. View **statistics** at the top of the page:\n   - Total actions over 30 days\n   - Breakdown by category\n   - Automated vs manual actions\n\n#### Compliance Benefits\n\nThis log enables you to:\n- ✅ **Respond to regulatory requests** with complete history\n- ✅ **Prove compliance** in case of audit\n- ✅ **Trace actions** on personal data\n- ✅ **Audit automated processes**\n- ✅ **Identify anomalies** in consent processing\n\n> **Note**: The log is read-only and cannot be modified. Entries are retained for a duration compliant with legal requirements.\n\n---\n\n## Interface and Navigation\n\n### Visual Themes\n\n- **Light Mode**: Bright interface\n- **Dark Mode**: Dark interface, reduces eye strain\n\nClick the sun/moon icon in the top bar to change theme.\n\n### Languages\n\nThe application is available in:\n- Français\n- English\n\nUse the language selector in the top bar.\n\n### Navigation Structure\n\n#### Side Menu\n\n**Main Section**:\n- Home (dashboard)\n- Resume Library\n- Missions\n- Adaptations\n- Resume Templates\n\n**Admin Section** (if authorized):\n- Users\n- Security Logs\n- **GDPR Audit Log** (GDPR action audit)\n- Metrics\n- Settings\n\n#### Top Bar\n\n- Logo: Return to home\n- Theme: Toggle light/dark\n- Language: FR/EN\n- About: Information and changelog\n- Profile and logout\n\n---\n\n## Best Practices\n\n### 📄 Resume Management\n\n#### Import and Quality\n\n| Best Practice | Why |\n|---------------|-----|\n| **Use PDF format** | Better text extraction and preserved layout |\n| **Avoid complex tables** | AI extracts linear text better |\n| **Ensure text is selectable** | Scans (images) cannot be analyzed |\n| **Standard fonts** | Arial, Calibri, Times New Roman for better compatibility |\n\n#### Recommended Resume Structure\n\n```\n1. Header (name, contact, professional title)\n2. Professional summary (3-5 lines)\n3. Key skills (technical and functional)\n4. Professional experience (reverse chronological)\n5. Education and certifications\n6. Languages and additional information\n```\n\n#### After Analysis\n\n- **Check extracted text**: View the \"Original\" tab to ensure extraction is correct\n- **Review improvements**: AI suggests, you validate and personalize\n- **Use versions**: Create multiple versions for different contexts\n- **Complete missing information**: Candidate email, profile type, industry\n\n### 🎯 Missions and Matching\n\n#### Creating Effective Missions\n\n| Element | Advice |\n|---------|--------|\n| **Title** | Precise and searchable (e.g., \"Senior Java Developer - Banking\") |\n| **Description** | Detail context, responsibilities, and technical environment |\n| **Required skills** | List mandatory skills with expected level |\n| **Preferred skills** | Add \"nice to have\" to refine matching |\n| **Associated client** | Link mission to a client for better tracking |\n\n#### Optimizing Matching\n\n- **Adjust weights**: Adapt weights according to relative importance of criteria\n- **Update keywords**: Click \"Refresh\" after modifying the description\n- **Use Profile Matching**: Quickly identify best candidates for a mission\n\n### 👥 Client and Contact Management\n\n#### Effective Organization\n\n- **Qualify your clients**: Distinguish active clients, prospects, and inactive\n- **Fill in contacts**: Function, email, phone for each contact\n- **Identify primary contact**: Facilitate communications\n- **Update regularly**: Remove obsolete contacts\n\n#### Commercial Tracking\n\n- **Document interactions**: Use notes to record exchanges\n- **Track submissions**: View CV submission history by client\n- **Analyze conversions**: Identify most receptive clients\n\n### 📊 Recruitment Pipeline\n\n#### Pipeline Management\n\n| Stage | Recommended Action |\n|-------|-------------------|\n| **New** | Quickly qualify the candidate |\n| **Shortlisted** | Contact candidate within 48h |\n| **Interview scheduled** | Create interview with all information |\n| **Interview completed** | Document feedback immediately |\n| **Proposed** | Follow client decision |\n| **Accepted/Rejected** | Archive with reasons |\n\n#### Documentation\n\n- **Add notes at each stage**: Record decisions and feedback\n- **Schedule interviews quickly**: Create event as soon as confirmed\n- **Record interview outcomes**: Structured feedback to improve process\n\n### ✉️ CV Submission and Communication\n\n#### Preparation\n\n- **Customize your templates**: Adapt tone to your brand image\n- **Complete your profile**: Function and phone for professional signature\n- **Verify contact details**: Email before sending\n\n#### Sending Best Practices\n\n- **One CV per email**: Avoid impersonal bulk sends\n- **Review attached CV**: Check version and format before sending\n- **Use connected Gmail**: For automatic submission tracking\n- **Check history**: Avoid duplicate submissions\n\n### 🔒 GDPR Compliance\n\n#### Consent Management\n\n| Action | When |\n|--------|------|\n| **Request consent** | As soon as CV with email is imported |\n| **Follow up if needed** | After 7 days without response |\n| **Respect refusals** | Anonymize or delete data |\n| **Document** | Keep record of consents |\n\n#### GDPR Best Practices\n\n- **Collect candidate email**: Essential for consent request\n- **Use official templates**: Compliant with legal requirements\n- **Monitor expirations**: Renew consents before expiration\n- **Audit regularly**: Check GDPR audit log\n\n### 📈 Market Radar and Intelligence\n\n#### Effective Use\n\n- **Check regularly**: Market data is updated periodically\n- **Compare regions**: Identify geographical opportunities\n- **Analyze trends**: Anticipate market changes\n- **Cross-reference with missions**: Adapt searches to market realities\n\n### ⚙️ Administration and Configuration\n\n#### For Administrators\n\n- **Configure AI prompts**: Adapt instructions to your business\n- **Adjust evaluation weights**: Prioritize criteria important to you\n- **Manage users**: Assign correct roles (admin, user)\n- **Customize templates**: Email and export adapted to your brand\n\n#### Security\n\n- **Change default passwords**: On first login\n- **Enable 2FA**: Two-factor authentication for sensitive accounts\n- **Monitor logs**: Detect suspicious activity\n- **Log out**: At end of session on shared computers\n\n### 🔄 Recommended Workflow\n\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│                    OPTIMAL WORKFLOW                          │\n├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\n│                                                              │\n│  1. IMPORT CV                                                │\n│     └─→ PDF format, selectable text                         │\n│                        ↓                                     │\n│  2. AUTOMATIC ANALYSIS                                       │\n│     └─→ Verify extraction, scores, tags                     │\n│                        ↓                                     │\n│  3. AI IMPROVEMENT                                           │\n│     └─→ Review, personalize, validate                       │\n│                        ↓                                     │\n│  4. GDPR CONSENT                                             │\n│     └─→ Send request if email available                     │\n│                        ↓                                     │\n│  5. MISSION MATCHING                                         │\n│     └─→ Identify compatible missions                        │\n│                        ↓                                     │\n│  6. CV ADAPTATION                                            │\n│     └─→ Generate version adapted to mission                 │\n│                        ↓                                     │\n│  7. CLIENT SUBMISSION                                        │\n│     └─→ Personalized email with adapted CV                  │\n│                        ↓                                     │\n│  8. PIPELINE TRACKING                                        │\n│     └─→ Document each stage until placement                 │\n│                                                              │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n### 💡 Productivity Tips\n\n| Tip | Benefit |\n|-----|---------|\n| **Use keyboard shortcuts** | Faster navigation |\n| **Filter effectively** | Quickly find relevant CVs |\n| **Create reusable templates** | Save time on submissions |\n| **Consult the chatbot** | Immediate contextual assistance |\n| **Export regularly** | Back up important data |\n\n---\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### Common Issues\n\n#### Resume Won't Import\n\n**Possible causes**:\n- Unsupported format (use PDF or DOCX)\n- Corrupted or password-protected file\n- Size exceeds 50 MB\n- Scanned PDF (image without text)\n\n**Solutions**:\n- Check file format\n- Re-export the resume from the original application\n- Prefer a PDF with selectable text\n- If you need to process scanned PDFs, ask your administrator to verify that the OCR server toolchain is installed (`tesseract` + `pdftoppm`)\n\n#### Analysis Takes Too Long\n\n**Normal time**: 10-30 seconds\n\n**If > 1 minute**:\n- Refresh the page\n- Check your internet connection\n- Retry the analysis\n\n#### Scores Seem Incorrect\n\n- Check that the resume is well-structured\n- View the extracted text (Original tab)\n- Reorganize the resume if needed and rerun analysis\n\n#### Cannot Log In\n\n- Check your credentials (email and password)\n- Verify your account is active\n- Contact an administrator\n\n#### PDF Export Not Working\n\n- Verify the improved resume is generated\n- Try a different browser\n- Disable pop-up blockers\n\n### Reporting a Bug\n\n1. Note what you were doing and the error message\n2. Take a screenshot if possible\n3. Contact your administrator\n\n### Performance Tips\n\n- Use Chrome, Firefox, or Edge (recent versions)\n- Stable internet connection\n- Clear cache if display issues occur\n\n---\n\n## FAQ\n\n### General Questions\n\n**Q: How many resumes can I import?**\nA: No limit. You can import as many resumes as needed.\n\n**Q: Is my data secure?**\nA: Yes, all data is stored securely and accessible only to authorized users.\n\n**Q: Does the application work offline?**\nA: No, an internet connection is required.\n\n### Analysis Questions\n\n**Q: How does the AI analyze resumes?**\nA: The AI uses advanced language models to extract, analyze, and evaluate content according to recruitment best practices.\n\n**Q: Can I modify evaluation criteria?**\nA: Administrators can adjust category weights in settings.\n\n**Q: Why is my ATS score low?**\nA: A low ATS score typically indicates complex formatting, lack of keywords, or non-standard structure.\n\n### Anonymous Mode Questions\n\n**Q: What is anonymous mode?**\nA: Anonymous mode replaces candidate personal information with a trigram (3 letters) and removes contact details.\n\n**Q: How is the trigram generated?**\nA: The trigram consists of the first letter of the first name and the first two letters of the last name (e.g., \"JDO\" for John Doe).\n\n**Q: Can I switch between nominative and anonymous mode?**\nA: Yes, the administrator can change the mode in settings. The change applies to new analyses and improvements.\n\n### Chatbot Questions\n\n**Q: I don't see the chatbot button, why?**\nA: The assistant can be disabled by an administrator. Contact your administrator to enable it.\n\n**Q: Are conversations saved?**\nA: No, conversations are cleared when you close the chat window.\n\n### Technical Questions\n\n**Q: What browsers are supported?**\nA: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge (recent versions).\n\n**Q: Is the application mobile-friendly?**\nA: Yes, the interface adapts to tablets and smartphones.\n\n---\n\n## Roadmap\n\nResumeConverter is constantly evolving, and **you are at the heart of this evolution**.\n\n### Listening to You\n\nThe next features of the application will be defined **based on your needs and feedback**. We believe that the best improvements come from those who use the tool every day.\n\n**How to participate?**\n\n- **Share your ideas**: What features would save you time?\n- **Report difficulties**: What could be simpler or more intuitive?\n- **Suggest evolutions**: How could the application better meet your business needs?\n\nEvery suggestion is carefully reviewed. Don't hesitate to contact your administrator or use the AI assistant to share your ideas!\n\n### Continuous Fixes and Improvements\n\nThe team is constantly working on:\n\n- **Bug Fixes**: Quick resolution of issues reported by users\n- **User Experience Enhancement**: More intuitive and fluid interface\n- **Performance Optimizations**: An ever more responsive application\n\n### Your Feedback is Valuable!\n\nWe strongly encourage users to share their comments, criticisms and suggestions for improvement. Every piece of feedback helps make the application better for everyone.\n\n**Together, let's build the tool that fits your needs!**\n\n---\n\n## Glossary\n\n**ATS (Applicant Tracking System)**: Candidate tracking system used by recruiters to automatically filter resumes.\n\n**Adaptation**: Customized version of a resume optimized for a specific mission.\n\n**Trigram**: 3-letter code representing a candidate in anonymous mode.\n\n**Match Score**: Measure (0-100) of the correspondence between a profile and a mission.\n\n**LLM (Large Language Model)**: AI language processing model (GPT-4o, Claude, etc.).\n\n**Nominative Mode**: Mode where the resume retains all personal information.\n\n**Anonymous Mode**: Mode where personal information is replaced by a trigram.\n\n**Soft Skills**: Behavioral competencies (communication, leadership, etc.).\n\n**Hard Skills**: Measurable technical competencies (programming, languages, etc.).\n\n---\n\n## Support\n\n### Getting Help\n\n1. **AI Assistant**: Available 24/7 via the chat button\n2. **This guide**: Complete documentation\n3. **Administrator**: For questions specific to your organization\n\n### Resources\n\n- **Changelog**: View updates in \"About\"\n- **API Documentation**: Swagger available for developers\n\n---\n\n**Last updated**: Version 1.7.0 - March 2026\n\n**Recent updates**:\n- **Selection Pipeline**: Complete candidate selection process tracking with configurable stages\n- **Interview Scheduling**: Client/partner interview management with Google Calendar integration\n- **Pipeline History**: Full traceability of stage changes and notes\n- **Integrated AI Suggestions**: Improvement recommendations displayed directly in the resume editor\n- **Full GDPR Compliance**: Automated consent management for external resumes\n- **GDPR Badge**: Visual consent status indicator on each resume\n- **MJML Email Templates**: Visual template editor with blocks\n- **Clients & Prospects**: Commercial portfolio with contacts and send history\n- **Email CV Sending**: Gmail draft creation with CV attachment and custom templates\n\n**Previous versions**:\n- v1.6.x: GDPR compliance, automatic consent sending, automatic CV deletion\n- v1.6.0: Company Logo, Enhanced User Profiles, Extended Email Keywords\n- v1.5.x: PostgreSQL migration, enhanced security, security logs, Swagger/OpenAPI\n- v1.4.x: Complete Market Radar, Tags management, ROME 4.0 reference\n- v1.3.0: Anonymous CV mode with trigram, progress overlay\n- v1.2.x: Profile matching, Market Radar France, URL navigation\n\n---\n\n*For any questions about this documentation, contact your system administrator.*\n"
